name: security-scanning-security-hardening
description: Coordinate multi-layer security scanning and hardening across application, infrastructure, and compliance controls.
capabilities: Coordinate multi-phase security assessments using SAST, DAST, and dependency scanning tools
Implement vulnerability remediation with prioritized fixes based on CVSS scores
Deploy defense-in-depth controls across frontend, backend, and infrastructure layers
Configure authentication and authorization with OAuth2, MFA, and RBAC
Validate compliance against OWASP ASVS, CIS Benchmarks, SOC2, and other frameworks
Establish security monitoring with SIEM integration and automated incident response
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 11 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is a legitimate defensive security orchestration tool for DevSecOps workflows. References to Metasploit, CIS Benchmarks, AES-256, and mobile security terms are all standard security industry practices. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: Orchestrate comprehensive security scanning, vulnerability remediation, and defense-in-depth hardening across your application, infrastructure, and compliance controls with automated DevSecOps workflows.
limitations: Does not perform actual security testing - only coordinates agents that execute scans
Requires existing authorization and approval for security testing activities
Cannot access external scanning tools or penetration testing services directly
Compliance validation provides guidance but does not replace formal audits

useCases.title: DevSecOps Security Automation
useCases.description: Integrate automated security scanning and hardening into CI/CD pipelines to catch vulnerabilities early and maintain continuous security posture.

useCases.title: Application Security Hardening
useCases.description: Apply comprehensive security controls to applications including input validation, encryption, authentication hardening, and secure configuration.

useCases.title: Compliance Preparation
useCases.description: Prepare for security compliance audits by implementing controls and generating documentation for OWASP, SOC2, CIS, and other frameworks.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Security Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the security-scanning-security-hardening skill to perform a quick vulnerability scan on my application. Focus on identifying critical issues and provide a prioritized remediation list.
promptTemplates.scenario: Initial security check for a new project

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Security Hardening
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the security-scanning-security-hardening skill to implement comprehensive security hardening for my application. Include vulnerability assessment, backend security controls, frontend security measures, and compliance validation.
promptTemplates.scenario: Full security implementation for production application

promptTemplates.title: Compliance Framework Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the security-scanning-security-hardening skill to validate my application against OWASP ASVS Level 2 and CIS Benchmarks. Generate a compliance gap analysis and remediation plan.

promptTemplates.title: Incident Response Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the security-scanning-security-hardening skill to establish security monitoring and SIEM integration for my infrastructure. Configure automated incident response playbooks for critical security events.

bestPractices: Run security assessments in non-production environments first before applying changes to production
Always maintain rollback plans when implementing security hardening changes
Prioritize remediation based on CVSS scores and business impact, not just automated findings
antiPatterns: Do not skip authorization checks - always verify you have permission before running security scans
Do not apply security changes without testing in a staging environment first
Do not rely solely on automated scanning - combine with manual security review for critical systems

faq.question: Does this skill perform actual penetration testing?
faq.answer: No, this skill orchestrates security workflows and coordinates agents. Actual penetration testing requires licensed tools and authorized testers.

faq.question: Can this skill scan external systems without authorization?
faq.answer: No, the skill includes guidance to avoid intrusive testing without approval. Always ensure you have explicit authorization before scanning any system.

faq.question: What compliance frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports validation against OWASP ASVS, CIS Benchmarks, SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS frameworks.

faq.question: Does this skill require any specific tools to be installed?
faq.answer: The skill coordinates AI agents that may use tools like Semgrep, OWASP ZAP, Snyk, or GitLeaks for scanning. Tool availability depends on your environment.

faq.question: Can this skill break my production application?
faq.answer: Security hardening changes can impact functionality. Always test in non-production environments first and maintain rollback plans as recommended in the skill.

faq.question: How long does a comprehensive security hardening take?
faq.answer: The skill supports quick, standard, and comprehensive scanning modes. Comprehensive hardening across all phases may take hours to days depending on application complexity.
